source_refs.label: ePrint 2013/676
source_refs.location: Fact 1, Fact 2, Table 1, Sect. 4 / Appendix A.1

component_scope.usage_notes: The same 4x4 S-box is used in the encryption round and in the top nibble of the 80-bit key schedule.

resolver_metadata.matching_notes: This pack is the current repository-side local-semantics artifact for PRESENT 4-bit xor-differential S-box activity / CDP modeling.

variable_layout.indexing_notes: The paper states that x3 and y3 are the least significant bits.

rule_groups.purpose: MILP tightening for PRESENT S-box differential propagation in the related-key security-evaluation lane used by Appendix A.1.

inequalities.label: cdp_1
inequalities.derivation: Fact 1(i): if the input difference is 1001 then the least significant output bit must be 0.

inequalities.label: cdp_2
inequalities.derivation: Fact 1(ii): if the input difference is 0001 then the least significant output bit must be 1.

inequalities.label: cdp_3
inequalities.derivation: Fact 1(ii): if the input difference is 1000 then the least significant output bit must be 1.

inequalities.label: cdp_4
inequalities.derivation: Fact 1(iii): if the output difference is 0001 then the least significant input bit must be 1.

inequalities.label: cdp_5
inequalities.derivation: Fact 1(iii): if the output difference is 0100 then the least significant input bit must be 1.

inequalities.label: cdp_6
inequalities.derivation: Fact 1(iv): if the output difference is 0101 then the least significant input bit must be 0.

notes: This rule pack intentionally captures only the CDP constraints used in the logical-condition modeling lane. The selected 6 convex-hull inequalities from Table 3 are not yet encoded because Appendix A.1 explicitly uses the Sect. 3.1 logical-condition lane for the chosen PRESENT-80 security-bound claim.
